title: medicago orientalis subsp. anatolica
intro: Medicago orientalis subsp. anatolica, also known as Anatolian lucerne or big trefoil, is a subspecies of the Medicago orientalis species, a type of legume or clover plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to protect against microbial infections. The health benefits of Medicago orientalis subsp. anatolica include its potential to help manage blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders such as diarrhea and constipation. In traditional herbal medicine, the leaves and flowers of this plant are often prepared as teas or infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it may also be used in the form of dried extracts or capsules.
